items.title: Negative Permian Prices Endure as Bidweek Natural Gas Trading Gets Rolling
items.summary: Elevated natural gas production and insufficient pipeline capacity in the Permian Basin resulted in backed up supply and pummeled West Texas prices on the first day of April bidweek trading on Wednesday.

items.title: EQT Sees Stronger Appalachian Pricing as Power, LNG Drive New Demand Wave
Futures Settle: Natural Gas Prices Gain as War Risks Offset Mildest March in 14 Years
items.summary: Natural gas futures edged higher Wednesday, reversing earlier losses as Tehran’s rejection of a U.S. peace plan kept war risks in play, with LNG feed gas demand and bullish storage expectations also supporting prices.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Natural Gas Cash Prices Pull Back
items.summary: Natural gas spot prices declined overall as the market segued into afternoon trading with weather demand tapered and production ample by spring standards.

items.title: How the Qatari LNG Outage is Disrupting Global Supply Flows
items.summary: The LNG market is staring down a deepening supply shortfall as war in the Middle East drags on with no end in sight and Qatar remains sidelined by the conflict, threatening to upend energy flows and crush some demand in the weeks ahead.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Natural Gas Prices Turbulent Amid War Volatility
items.summary: Natural gas futures tracked the global oil market in choppy trading as of midday on Wednesday, as the war in Iran continued to infuse volatility.

items.title: Cheniere CEO Warns of Coming LNG Squeeze as Corpus Christi Train 5 Nears Operation
items.summary: Cheniere Energy Inc. CEO Jack Fusco said the company is working to commission Train 5 of its latest Corpus Christi LNG expansion as U.S. exporters push to fill a widening global supply gap.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Oversupply Crushing Western Natural Gas Spot Prices
items.summary: While Nymex natural gas futures have taken their cues from global LNG and oil price trends amid the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, physical pricing in the day-ahead U.S. natural gas market is reflecting an increasingly bearish near-term outlook heading

items.title: Middle East War Could Push West Canada’s LNG Projects Forward

items.title: Gas Crisis Dwarfs Oil Shock as LNG Supply Breaks
items.summary: Until less than a month ago, most energy analysts were warning about a glut in oil and a likely glut in LNG as new capacity came online in the United States. Glut was the word that described global energy market fundamentals. Then the U.S. and Israel

items.title: Crude Drops 4% on Iran News Just as Dallas Fed Signals Oil Rebound
items.summary: A sharp rebound in U.S. oilfield activity is colliding with a sudden geopolitical shock, as the latest Dallas Fed Energy Survey shows energy firms swinging back into expansion just as crude prices plunge roughly 4% on headlines that President Donald

items.title: Russia's Vital Baltic Oil Hubs Crippled by Ukrainian Drone Campaign
items.summary: A major fire broke out at the Ust-Luga oil port in Russia’s Leningrad region following a large-scale Ukrainian drone attack overnight on March 25, the second critical Baltic Sea export hub to be set ablaze this week following a similar strike on the

items.title: US Oil Inventories Continue to Climb While Gasoline Inventories Shrink
items.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States increased by 6.9 million barrels during the week ending March 20, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The increase brings commercial stockpiles

items.title: Japan Urges IEA to Prepare for Second Emergency Oil Release
items.summary: Japan’s Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi on Wednesday asked the head of the International Energy Agency (IEA) to be ready for an additional release of oil stocks from reserves if it is necessary.  The IEA two weeks ago launched the biggest coordinated e

items.title: Will the Iran War Revive Russia’s Power of Siberia 2 Pipeline?
